# Restaurants / Food Service Industry Snapshot — TheDealSheet

**Author:** TheDealSheet Editorial
**Source:** [thedealsheet.co](https://thedealsheet.co/industries/restaurant)
**Saved:** 2026-04-20

## Why I Saved This

Cross-reference for G&B's Specialty Coffee Equipment Servicing niche. Restaurants are an adjacent-customer segment (fine dining, hotel F&B) that consumes high-end coffee equipment. Captured to test whether TDS surfaces equipment-service signals that affect G&B's thesis — e.g., PE consolidation of restaurant chains impacting authorized-dealer service demand, fine-dining growth/contraction affecting specialty coffee shop universe.

Companion snapshot to `[[library/external/2026-04-20-thedealsheet-commercial-cleaning]]`. Part of the 2026-04-20 TDS PE-POV cross-reference sweep — see `[[traces/agents/2026-04-20-tds-pe-pov-xref]]`.

## Content

### Market Size & Growth
- U.S. market: $1.55T projected 2026 sales (National Restaurant Association)
- CAGR: 5.93% (2026–2034, IMARC); Full-service 11.07% CAGR 2026-2031 (Mordor)
- Real sales growth only 0.8-1.3% after inflation adjustments
- YTD 2025 M&A: 32 deals (PE add-ons -28.9% YoY per Capstone)

### Revenue Mix
- Limited-Service (QSR/Fast Casual): 36%
- Full-Service Restaurants: 36.4% (independents control 78.6% of segment)
- Other Food Service: 27%

### Valuation Multiples
| Revenue Band | Multiple | Metric |
|---|---|---|
| $250K-$500K | 1.2-1.5x | SDE |
| $500K-$1M | 1.5-2.2x | SDE |
| $1M-$2.5M | 2.0-2.5x | SDE |
| $2.5M-$10M | 2.3-3.0x | SDE |
| $10M+ | 3.5-4.5x+ | EBITDA |

PE pays 8-11x EBITDA for high-growth franchisors (Dave's Hot Chicken $1.1B, Jersey Mike's to Blackstone). Multiple arbitrage spread: independents at 1.5-2.5x → platforms at 3-4x+.

### Unit Economics
- Net margin: 3-9% industry average (>10% top quartile)
- Prime cost (COGS + labor): 60-65%
- Food cost: 28-35%
- Labor cost: 30-35%
- Off-premises revenue: 20-30% (>35% top quartile)
- Labor turnover: 75-80% ($5,864 replacement cost)
- Delivery platform commissions compress margins 20-30%

### PE Consolidation — Notable Platforms
- **Roark Capital** — Dave's Hot Chicken ($1.1B+), emerging QSR/fast casual
- **Blackstone** — Jersey Mike's, asset-light franchisor platforms
- **Franchise Equity Partners** — 7 Brew Coffee, Bojangles franchisee roll-ups
- **Thompson Street Capital** — Cava (400→550+ units), fast casual scaling
- **Eyas Capital** — Bojangles largest franchisee

33% of all restaurant M&A is PE-backed.

### Key Growth Drivers
- Digital ordering / delivery now 20-30% of sales (12.38% CAGR through 2031)
- Technology adoption: 54% use AI menu analysis; POS reducing waste 10-15%; Olo/DoorDash tech M&A $6.1B combined 2025
- Casual dining resurgence vs QSR (full-service capturing traffic as QSR pricing gap narrows)
- Micro-concepts & ghost kitchens enabling <$100K launches

### Risks
- Labor cost inflation (4-6% YoY wage growth, 80% turnover)
- Thin margins (3-9% average, limited pricing power)
- Delivery platform dependency (20-30% commissions)
- Food cost volatility (95% of operators citing increases; tariff pressure)
- Real estate pressure (rent 15% of revenue in urban markets vs 6-8% ideal)
- Consumer traffic softness (below pre-pandemic levels)

### Top Geographic Markets
Austin TX, Nashville TN, Phoenix AZ, Charlotte NC, Tampa FL, Denver CO, Raleigh-Durham, San Antonio, Orlando, Las Vegas. Sunbelt population migration driving 1.5-2.7% annual growth.

### TDS Coverage of Equipment / Service Angle
**Zero direct coverage of commercial-kitchen equipment servicing, coffee-equipment service networks, or authorized-dealer maintenance contracts.**

Equipment-adjacent mentions:
- "Deferred maintenance, equipment nearing end-of-life" listed as lower-multiple factor (negative valuation signal)
- "Tech stack" / "POS integration" framed as operator-side upgrade, not service-vendor opportunity
- Worcester MA listing: kitchen equipment treated as asset-embedded (not service contract)
- No mention of La Marzocco, espresso equipment, or specialty coffee equipment anywhere

TDS frames restaurants purely as operator/buyer economics — the service-vendor ecosystem (HVAC, hood cleaning, refrigeration service, coffee equipment service) is invisible in their PE-POV lens.

## Key Patterns

- Pattern 1: TDS editorial POV is operator-as-buyer, not service-vendor-as-buyer — restaurant page does NOT surface the equipment-service ecosystem G&B is playing in
- Pattern 2: PE consolidation is concentrated in QSR/fast casual (Roark, Blackstone, Franchise Equity Partners) — NOT in fine dining or specialty coffee shops, which remain 78.6% independent
- Pattern 3: Franchise Equity Partners bought 7 Brew Coffee — first signal of PE interest in drive-thru coffee concept, but this is a brand play (not equipment servicing)
- Pattern 4: "Deferred maintenance / equipment end-of-life" is a valuation discount factor, implying operators underinvest in equipment upkeep — potential tailwind for scheduled-service providers
- Pattern 5: Full-service casual dining 11.07% CAGR forecast implies expanding installed base of commercial-grade equipment needing service
- Pattern 6: Ghost kitchens / micro-concepts launching for <$100K rely on used/leased equipment — less service-contract penetration

## How I Might Use This

**Coffee equipment servicing implications:**

1. **Customer segment health check** — TDS signals fine dining and independent full-service are NOT being consolidated by PE. G&B's specialty coffee shop + fine dining + luxury hotel customer base remains fragmented → authorized-dealer service relationships stay decentralized → G&B's acquisition thesis (fragmented service providers serving fragmented customers) holds.

2. **Threat-adjacent watch: Franchise Equity Partners + 7 Brew Coffee** — PE roll-up of drive-thru coffee chains could eventually centralize equipment procurement/service contracts away from independent authorized dealers. Not a near-term threat (7 Brew uses its own equipment stack, not specialty espresso OEMs), but worth tracking if roll-ups expand to specialty concepts.

3. **Deferred maintenance as demand driver** — TDS flagging equipment-end-of-life as a valuation discount implies restaurant operators under-invest in scheduled maintenance. Coffee equipment is different (shop revenue dies immediately when the La Marzocco breaks) but the broader kitchen-equipment market has chronic deferred-maintenance debt — adjacent opportunity for commercial-kitchen equipment servicing sub-niche (hoods, refrigeration, combi ovens) with similar economics to coffee equipment servicing (OEM authorization, scheduled PM contracts, emergency dispatch).

4. **NOT relevant to G&B** — Restaurant ownership/operator acquisition. Already excluded per session decisions (bad end-customer economics, thin margins, labor intensity).

**Adjacent opportunity flag:** Commercial kitchen equipment servicing (broader than coffee) — authorized-dealer service networks for Hobart, Vulcan, True, Hoshizaki, Rational. Same structural DNA as coffee equipment servicing (OEM authorization moat, scheduled PM recurring revenue, emergency service pricing power), but 10-20x the installed base. Worth flagging to niche-intelligence for evaluation.
